ogTitle: Invermay vs Jackeys Marsh: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Invermay and Jackeys Marsh.
heading: Invermay vs Jackeys Marsh — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Invermay, Tasmania and Jackeys Marsh, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jackeys Marsh has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Invermay (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Invermay or Jackeys Marsh safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Invermay has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Jackeys Marsh scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Invermay scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Jackeys Marsh scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Invermay or Jackeys Marsh?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jackeys Marsh has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
